title: floor_check and running_low tools are stubs but _INVENTORY_AGENT_STUBBED=False, causing the dispatcher to route inventory queries into a broken agent
evidence:
_INVENTORY_AGENT_STUBBED = False  # stock_keeper.py:82

return json.dumps({
    "error": "floor_check is in stub state",  # agent_tools.py:152-158
    ...
})

_stub_flag = getattr(inventory_agent_module, "_INVENTORY_AGENT_STUBBED", False)  # chat.py:2139
if _stub_flag:  # always False, stub fallback never triggers
fix: Either (a) make chat.py's dispatcher call `_floor_check_is_workshop_stub()` (already implemented in atelier_observatory.py) to detect the real stub state, or (b) set `_INVENTORY_AGENT_STUBBED = True` in the workshop starter version of stock_keeper.py so the flag truthfully reflects the tool state. O

title: graph_orchestrator.py uses a stale import path (strands.agent.graph) that does not exist in the installed Strands SDK, causing GRAPH_AVAILABLE to always be False
evidence:
from strands.agent.graph import GraphBuilder  # type: ignore  # graph_orchestrator.py:18
# ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'strands.agent.graph'  (verified at runtime)

GRAPH_AVAILABLE = False  # stays False, always
'graph_builder_available': GRAPH_AVAILABLE,  # line 87
fix:
Change the import in graph_orchestrator.py to match the working import in graph_pattern.py:
```python
from strands.multiagent import GraphBuilder  # type: ignore
```
This will cause GRAPH_AVAILABLE to be set correctly and the Atelier visualization panel will report the true status.

title: GraphAgentAdapter: when the router node fails (NodeResult contains an Exception), all five conditional edges evaluate to False silently with no logged warning
fix: Add a check inside the condition closure: if `router_result.status == Status.FAILED` (or `isinstance(router_result.result, Exception)`), log a warning at WARNING level and return False. Alternatively, check `node_result.status` in the `__call__` loop and emit a warning before returning `_EmptyResult

title: Pattern I (agents_as_tools): specialist @tool wrappers swallow all exceptions and return error JSON, but the orchestrator's Haiku router may include the error text as its own reply to the user
fix: Return a user-friendly, intent-neutral error string instead of exposing internal exception details. For example: `return json.dumps({"error": "I ran into a problem searching right now. Try again in a moment.", "internal_detail": str(e)})`. Add `"internal_detail"` for telemetry but keep `"error"` gen

title: graph_orchestrator.py node metadata shows incorrect model labels (Opus 4.6 for support/search/recommendation) that contradict actual agent configuration which is env-overridable
fix: Replace hardcoded strings with values derived from `settings.BEDROCK_OPUS_MODEL` / `settings.BEDROCK_HAIKU_MODEL` at call time, e.g., `"model": settings.BEDROCK_OPUS_MODEL`. Import `settings` from `config` at the top of graph_orchestrator.py.

title: agentcore_evals.py uses wrong boto3 service — bedrock-agentcore has no create_evaluation_job
fix: Change the client to boto3.client("bedrock", ...) and rewrite the payload to match the bedrock CreateEvaluationJob schema: jobName, roleArn (not jobRoleArn), evaluationConfig (with automated or human branch), inferenceConfig (with models or ragConfigs), and outputDataConfig.

title: managed_policy._region() calls settings.get_aws_region() which does not exist — always falls back
fix: Replace settings.get_aws_region() with settings.aws_region_resolved (the actual property name defined at config.py:261).

title: IndexPerformanceService uses blocking psycopg.connect inside async route handlers — event loop stalls
evidence:
async def compare_index_performance(...):
    ...
    with psycopg.connect(self.conn_string) as conn:  # blocking connect on event loop
        results["dataset_size"] = await self._get_dataset_size(conn)  # awaits sync code
        results["hnsw"] = await self._test_hnsw_index(conn, ...)     # 4 blocking queries
        results["sequential"] = await self._test_sequential_scan(conn, ...)  # 2 bloc
fix: Either (a) switch to `psycopg.AsyncConnection` and `async with` cursor throughout the service, or (b) wrap the entire blocking body in `asyncio.to_thread(lambda: self._run_sync_benchmark(...))` and move the inner logic to a synchronous method.

title: vector_search.py vector_search_filtered: first bind construction (line 208) is immediately overwritten — dead code
evidence:
bind: List[Any] = [embedding] + params[1:] + [limit]   # built on line 208
# Recreate ``params[0]`` was the embedding placeholder for clause
# accounting; the actual SQL has the embedding twice (once in the
# CTE, once in the SELECT/ORDER BY through the CTE alias). Simpler
# to rebuild ``bind`` cleanly:
bind = [embedding]   # overwrites line 208 completely on line 213
fix: Delete lines 208–212 (the first bind construction and its misleading comment). Keep only the second construction starting at line 213. Optionally correct the comment to clarify that the CTE is referenced by alias, requiring only one embedding binding.

title: business_logic.py module docstring lists non-existent column image_verified
evidence:
Aligned to the boutique catalog schema:
    productId, name, brand, color, price, description, category, tags,
    rating, reviews (TEXT), "imgUrl", badge, tier, image_verified,
    quantity, embedding, created_at, updated_at

# 001_schema.sql actual columns: no image_verified; reviews integer
fix: Remove `image_verified` from the docstring column list. Change `reviews (TEXT)` to `reviews integer` to match the DDL.

title: Process-global `ContextManager` mixes messages from all concurrent sessions
evidence: `get_context_manager()` lines 580-585: global `_context_manager` singleton, no session key. `clear_context` in app.py line 972 calls `context_manager.clear_context()` ignoring the `session_id` query param.
fix: Either (a) key the context manager by `session_id` (a dict of `ContextManager` instances), or (b) document explicitly that this is a process-level aggregate counter only, and fix the `clear_context` endpoint to not accept a misleading `session_id` parameter.

title: Client-controlled `conversation_history[].role` field flows unsanitized into LLM prompt
evidence: chat.py lines 626-630: `role = msg.get('role', 'user')` then `conversation_context += f"{role.upper()}: {content}\n\n"`. models/search.py line 89: `role: str` (no validation).
fix: Add a Literal validator to `ChatMessage.role` (e.g., `Literal["user", "assistant"]`) so Pydantic rejects arbitrary values at the request boundary. Alternatively, normalize `role` to the safe set before concatenation.

title: User email logged at INFO level on every chat turn
evidence: Line 534: `logger.info(f"💬 Enhanced chat processing: '...' (mode={workshop_mode or 'agentic'}, user={user.get('email') if user else 'anonymous'})")`. Line 608: `"session.user": user.get("email", "anonymous")` set as an OTEL trace attribute.
fix: Replace email with a privacy-safe identifier (e.g., Cognito `sub` or a hash of the email) in log lines and OTEL attributes.

title: `tool_audit_writer.record_allow` docstring incorrectly describes caller
evidence: tool_audit_writer.py lines 106-107: `"""INSERT a row into pellier.tool_audit for an ALLOW decision. Called from an in-process AfterToolCall telemetry path..."""`. chat.py line 1871: `tool_audit_writer.record_allow(...)` called inside `on_before_tool`.
fix: Update the docstring to say 'Called from the BeforeToolCallEvent handler in chat.py.'

title: CORS wildcard + allow_credentials=True is invalid and rejected by browsers
evidence:
allow_origins=["*"],  # In enterprise deployments, specify actual origins
allow_credentials=True,
fix: Replace the wildcard with settings.cors_origins_list (already defined in config.py). If workshop attendees need to add their own origin, they extend CORS_ORIGINS in .env.

title: POST /api/tools/restock is an unauthenticated DB write endpoint
fix: Add `user=Depends(get_current_user)` and gate on `user is not None`, or remove the endpoint if it is only needed via the agent path. Replace `request: dict` with a Pydantic model that enforces `product_id: int` and `quantity: int` with bounds, so missing/invalid fields return a 422 instead of a 500.

title: services/auth.py verify_cognito_token leaks internal JWT parse error details to the caller
evidence:
except jwt.InvalidTokenError as e:
    raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail=f"Invalid token: {e}")
fix: Change the detail to the same opaque string the rest of the codebase uses: `detail="auth_failed"`. Log the raw exception message at DEBUG level instead.

title: config.py CORS_ORIGINS list is defined but never applied; app.py hardcodes wildcard
evidence:
CORS_ORIGINS: list[str] = [
    "http://localhost:5173",
    "http://localhost:3000",
    ...
]
# in app.py:
allow_origins=["*"],
fix: Replace the hardcoded wildcard with `settings.cors_origins_list` so the .env file controls the allowed origins.

title: delete_cookie() in auth.py does not pass secure/samesite attributes — cookie may not be cleared in some browsers
fix: Pass the same attributes used during set_cookie: `response.delete_cookie(cookie, path="/", secure=True, samesite="lax")`. FastAPI's delete_cookie accepts these kwargs.

title: Many app.py exception handlers pass raw str(e) as the HTTP detail to the caller
evidence:
raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))
# e.g. at lines 563, 618, 632, 647, 665, 694, 831, 843, 892, 924, 1011
fix: Replace detail=str(e) with a generic message (e.g. "search_failed", "restock_failed") and log the real exception at ERROR level. The routes/search.py already follows this pattern (raises HTTPException 500, detail="search_failed").

title: Backend SKILL.md copies (pellier/backend/skills/*/SKILL.md) diverge from canonical skills/ copies loaded at runtime
evidence: diff pellier/backend/skills/the-gift-table/SKILL.md vs skills/the-gift-table/SKILL.md shows missing display_name field and entirely different body text. loader.py line 24: _DEFAULT_SKILLS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / 'skills' resolves to repo-root /skills/, not pellier/backend/skills/.
fix: Either (a) remove the pellier/backend/skills/*/SKILL.md sub-folders entirely (keeping only the Python modules in pellier/backend/skills/) and rely solely on /skills/*/SKILL.md, or (b) keep them as intentional starter-code stubs and add a clear comment at the top of each saying 'Starter stub — runtim

title: SkillRouter instantiated fresh per-request — agent cache on the instance is never reused across requests
evidence: chat.py line 1800: `router = SkillRouter(get_registry())` inside the per-request generator. router.py line 74: `self._agent = None` and line 94-109: lazy Agent construction with BedrockModel. strands/models/bedrock.py line 176: `self.client = session.client('bedrock-runtime', ...)` created in __init__.
fix: Cache the SkillRouter as a module-level or app-level singleton, similar to how the SkillRegistry is cached via get_registry(). Add a get_skill_router() function in skills/loader.py (or a new module) that lazily initializes and caches a SkillRouter wrapping the singleton registry. This reuses the Bed

title: SKILL.md example in models.py docstring references a non-existent skill name
evidence: models.py line 31: `description="Canonical name from frontmatter, e.g. 'style-advisor'."`. Actual skill names: the-gift-table, the-makers-shelf, the-packing-list.
fix: Change the example to `e.g. 'the-gift-table'` to match the current naming convention.

title: deploy_gateway.py header claims 13 tools reach Gateway but TOOL_SCHEMAS registers only 12
evidence:
# Header: "Together these surface every backend ``@tool`` (13 of 13) over Gateway"
# Actual count in TOOL_SCHEMAS:
#   search: semantic_search, find_pieces_hybrid, get_inventory_health, get_low_stock_products, restock_product (5)
#   pricing: find_deals, get_price_analysis, compare_products (3)
#   recommendation: get_recommendations, get_trending_products (2)
#   experience: process_return, escal
fix: Either identify and add the 13th tool to TOOL_SCHEMAS and its corresponding Lambda server, or update the header comment to '12 of 12' (or '12 of 13 — <tool> stays in-process because <reason>').

title: STACKNAME omitted from prereq validation loop; empty value causes opaque failure at step 8
evidence:
for _v in PGHOSTARN PGSECRET PGDATABASE AWS_REGION COGNITO_POOL COGNITO_CLIENT AGENTCORE_ROLE_ARN; do
    # STACKNAME is absent from this list
    ...
done
# later at line 386:
  --stack-name "$STACKNAME"  # empty string causes AWS CLI error
fix: Add STACKNAME to the for-loop on line 74: for _v in PGHOSTARN PGSECRET PGDATABASE AWS_REGION COGNITO_POOL COGNITO_CLIENT AGENTCORE_ROLE_ARN STACKNAME; do

title: deploy_lambda.py leaves package.zip artifact in the caller's working directory on every invocation
evidence:
with open('package.zip', 'wb') as z:
    z.write(zip_content)

zip_size_mb = len(zip_content) / (1024 * 1024)
...
os.remove(zip_path)  # removes the tmp file, NOT package.zip
fix: Remove the package.zip write entirely (the zip_content is already in memory for the Lambda API call), or write it to the tempdir with a function-name prefix: open(os.path.join(temp_dir, f'{function_name}.zip'), 'wb'). If it is intentionally kept for debugging, add a comment and ensure it is gitignor

title: Aurora cluster parameter group does not set search_path — unqualified table names fail in ad-hoc psql sessions
evidence:
ClusterParameterGroup:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBClusterParameterGroup
    Properties:
      Parameters:
        shared_preload_libraries: vector
        rds.force_ssl: "1"
fix: Add `search_path: '"$user", pellier, public'` to the ClusterParameterGroup Parameters block. This makes unqualified names resolve to `pellier.*` first while keeping backward compatibility.

title: check_model_access.py hardcodes REGION='us-east-1' with no env-var override
evidence:
REGION = "us-east-1"
...
client = boto3.client("bedrock-runtime", region_name=REGION)
print(f"Checking Bedrock model access in {REGION}...\n")
fix: Replace the constant with `REGION = os.environ.get('AWS_REGION', 'us-east-1')` and add a CLI `--region` argument. If the us-east-1 constraint is load-bearing (the comment explains it), keep it but add an assertion or clear warning when `AWS_REGION != 'us-east-1'`.

title: dry-run-builders.sh missing `set -e` — non-pipeline command failures are silently swallowed
evidence:
set -uo pipefail
...
restore() {
  if ! $KEEP && [[ -f "${TOOLS}.dryrun.bak" ]]; then
    mv "${TOOLS}.dryrun.bak" "$TOOLS"
    info "Restored original agent_tools.py (stub). Backend will reload."
  fi
}
fix: Change line 24 to `set -euo pipefail`. Review any commands that are intentionally allowed to fail and add `|| true` to those specific lines.

title: bootstrap-environment.sh .bashrc heredoc writes literal `$AWS_REGION` string (single-quoted EOF), resulting in a self-referencing no-op export
evidence:
cat >> "/home/$CODE_EDITOR_USER/.bashrc" << 'EOF'
# AWS Configuration
export AWS_REGION="$AWS_REGION"
export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION="$AWS_REGION"
fix: Either use an unquoted heredoc (`<< EOF`) so `$AWS_REGION` expands to the actual value, or remove the two export lines entirely since the `.env` source block immediately below already sets the region.

title: bootstrap-environment.sh logs 'Python 3.13 configured' regardless of which version was actually installed
evidence:
log "✅ Python 3.13 configured"  # line 713
echo "✅ Python 3.13 configured"  # line 836
fix: Replace both hardcoded strings with `log "✅ Python ${PY_VER} configured"` and `echo "✅ Python ${PY_VER} configured"` to match the version actually installed.

title: Module-level response cache in useAgentChat ignores persona/customer_id, serving one persona's personalized results to another
evidence: Lines 157-170: `const responseCache = new Map<...>()` at module level; `function cacheKey(query: string) { return query.trim().toLowerCase() }`. Line 699: `persona?.customer_id ?? null` is passed to the backend but not included in the cache key.
fix: Include the customer_id in the cache key: `function cacheKey(query: string, customerId: string | null | undefined) { return `${customerId ?? 'anon'}:${query.trim().toLowerCase()}` }`. Also clear the cache on persona sign-out as well as in clearChat.

title: STM hydration guard in useAgentChat reads stale `messages` via closure — could overwrite active conversation
evidence: useAgentChat.ts lines 286-313, especially line 297: `if (messages.length > 1) return` — `messages` is declared in outer scope but not listed in `}, [sessionId])` at line 313.
fix: Replace the direct `messages.length` read with the ref that is always in sync: `if (messagesRef.current.length > 1) return`. messagesRef is kept current by a separate useEffect (lines 326-328) and is safe to read asynchronously.

title: LayoutContext auto-start-tour useEffect has empty dependency array but closes over stale showOnboarding and workshopMode
evidence: LayoutContext.tsx lines 72-82: `useEffect(() => { if (showOnboarding) return; ... }, [])` — empty deps array captures showOnboarding at mount-time only.
fix: Add `showOnboarding` to the dependency array: `}, [showOnboarding])`. Also guard the timer launch with a ref to prevent re-triggering on subsequent showOnboarding changes after the initial transition. Alternatively, trigger startTour from the onboarding completion handler directly.

title: chat.ts SSE streaming fetch calls omit credentials: 'include', breaking cookie-based auth
evidence:
chat.ts lines 93-108: `await fetch(..., { method: 'POST', headers: getAuthHeaders(), body: ... })` — no `credentials: 'include'`.
api.ts line 78: `withCredentials: true` in the Axios client that is NOT used for streaming.
fix: Add `credentials: 'include'` to the fetch options in sendChatMessageStreaming (line 93) and sendChatMessage (line 176). The cookie will then be sent alongside the Bearer token (which may be absent in the C9 flow), and the backend can use whichever auth mechanism it finds.

title: useScrollAndFlash pending flash timeouts accumulate without cleanup on unmount
evidence: useScrollAndFlash.ts lines 36-51: `const pendingTimeouts = useRef<number[]>([])` declared, timers pushed via `.push(timer)`, but no useEffect with `return () => pendingTimeouts.current.forEach(clearTimeout)` exists.
fix: Add a cleanup useEffect: `useEffect(() => () => { pendingTimeouts.current.forEach(clearTimeout); pendingTimeouts.current = [] }, [])`. Also clear handled timers after they fire to prevent unbounded growth.

title: AtelierErrorBoundary never resets on intra-atelier navigation
evidence: AtelierFrame.tsx line 26-29: `<AtelierErrorBoundary><Outlet /></AtelierErrorBoundary>` — no key or location prop. AtelierErrorBoundary.tsx: `handleReset` is only called from the 'Return to Sessions' Link onClick.
fix: Key the boundary on the current location pathname so React remounts it on navigation: wrap AtelierErrorBoundary in a component that reads `useLocation().pathname` and passes it as the `key` prop, e.g. `<AtelierErrorBoundary key={location.pathname}>`. Alternatively, implement `componentDidUpdate(prev

title: PERSONA_TURN_TRACES[marco][0] incorrectly labels the-packing-list skill for Turn 1
evidence: personaCurations.ts line 397: `{ skill: 'the-packing-list', tools: ['find_pieces'] }` for index 0. session-marco-opening-demo.json Turn 1 meta has no `skill` field; Turn 2 meta line 142: `"skill": "the-packing-list"`.
fix: Change PERSONA_TURN_TRACES[marco][0] to `{ tools: ['find_pieces'] }` (no skill). Index 1 is correct with `{ skill: 'the-packing-list', tools: ['find_pieces', 'style_match'] }`.

title: elapsedMs mismatch between sessions.json index and session-detail fixtures — visible in Brief tab
evidence: sessions.json line 127: `"elapsedMs": 2140` for theo-ceramics-return. session-theo-ceramics-return.json line 5: `"elapsedMs": 5840`. BriefTab.tsx line 583: `{ label: 'Elapsed', value: formatElapsed(session.elapsedMs) }`.
fix: Reconcile the elapsedMs values: either update sessions.json to match the detail fixtures (5840 for theo-ceramics-return, 6231 for anna-birthday-gift) or vice versa. The detail fixture is the more authoritative source since it contains the full trace.

title: sortSessionsByRecency function name and file-level JSDoc contradict the implementation
evidence: Line 5 JSDoc: 'sorted by most recent first'. Line 30: function `sortSessionsByRecency`. Line 32: `(a, b) => new Date(a.timestamp).getTime() - new Date(b.timestamp).getTime()` (ascending = oldest first).
fix: Rename the function to `sortSessionsChronologically` (or `sortSessionsAscending`) and update the file-level JSDoc to read 'sorted chronologically (earliest first)' to match the implementation and the correct inner comment.

title: ChatTab AGENTS_LIST hardcodes Experience Guide as 'idle', contradicting agents.json 'shipped' status
evidence: ChatTab.tsx line 1582: `{ name: 'Experience Guide', status: 'idle' as const }`. agents.json line 41-47: `"name": "Experience Guide"`, `"status": "shipped"`.
fix: Change `status: 'idle'` to `status: 'live'` for Experience Guide in AGENTS_LIST to match agents.json. Alternatively, derive the agent list from the `useAtelierData('agents')` fixture instead of hardcoding it, so status stays in sync.

title: evaluations.json uses generic agentName aliases ('Search', 'Recommendation', 'Pricing', 'Inventory') that do not match the canonical agent names in agents.json
evidence: evaluations.json lines 3, 15, 27, 39: `"agentName": "Search"`, `"Recommendation"`, `"Pricing"`, `"Inventory"`. agents.json: `"Style Advisor"`, `"Curator"`, `"Value Analyst"`, `"Stock Keeper"`, `"Experience Guide"`.
fix: Align evaluations.json agentName values with agents.json canonical names: 'Search' → 'Style Advisor', 'Recommendation' → 'Curator', 'Pricing' → 'Value Analyst', 'Inventory' → 'Stock Keeper'. Add the missing 'Experience Guide' scorecard. This makes cross-surface navigation coherent for workshop atten

title: PersonaStrip in ChatTab.tsx declares openingQuery prop but never uses it
evidence: Line 85: `openingQuery: string;` in prop type. Line 86: `= ({ personaId }) => {` — openingQuery destructured out. Lines 90-145: no reference to openingQuery.
fix: Either remove `openingQuery` from the prop type and the call site, or actually render it (e.g. as a subtitle under the persona name in the strip).

title: AtelierFrame does not wrap AtelierFrame's own route with AuthGate despite the comment claiming it does
evidence: App.tsx line 172 comment: 'gated by AuthGate'. Line 182: `<Route path="/atelier" element={<AtelierFrame />}>` — no AuthGate wrapper in JSX.
fix: Either wrap the Atelier route: `<Route path="/atelier" element={<AuthGate><AtelierFrame /></AuthGate>}>` or update the comment to accurately reflect the current behavior (Cognito-gated only when env vars are set; otherwise open).

title: ToolChip uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML with un-sanitized backend HTML in panel.meta
evidence:
<span
  className="italic"
  style={{ color: c.muted }}
  dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: panel.meta }}
/>
fix: Strip tags on the frontend before rendering: use `stripTags(panel.meta)` (same helper already defined in ConfidenceSummary.tsx) and render as plain text, or use DOMPurify to allowlist only `<span>` with safe style attributes.

title: workshop.ts: non-null assertion on res.body will throw on environments where body can be null
evidence: const reader = res.body!.getReader()
fix: Guard explicitly: `if (!res.body) throw new Error('Response body is null — server may not support streaming');` before calling `getReader()`, so the `catch` block in the caller can surface a meaningful error message.

title: clearCart uses browser confirm() — blocks React render thread and breaks in SSR/test environments
evidence: const clearCart = useCallback(() => {\n    if (confirm('Are you sure you want to clear your cart?')) {
fix: Replace with an in-UI confirmation step: set a `pendingClear` boolean state, render a confirmation pill inline in CartPanel, and only call `setItems([])` when the user clicks the confirm pill. This keeps the interaction async and consistent with the rest of the component.

title: blurbForProduct uses a lookbehind assertion (?<=) that crashes on Safari < 16.4
evidence: const sentences = turn.content.split(/(?<=[.!?])\s+/);
fix: Replace with a lookahead-based split that is universally supported: `turn.content.split(/(?=[.!?]\s)/).map(s => s.trim()).filter(Boolean)` or a manual sentence splitter using `indexOf`.

title: ConciergeModal: role="dialog" without aria-modal="true" — screen readers may not trap virtual focus
evidence: role="dialog"\naria-label="Ask Pellier"\n// no aria-modal
fix: Add `aria-modal="true"` to both the ConciergeModal and ChatDrawer dialog containers.

title: ConciergeModal: onKeyPress is deprecated in React 17+ / DOM spec and silently dropped in some browsers
evidence: onKeyPress={handleKeyPress}  // ConciergeModal.tsx:697
fix: Replace `onKeyPress` with `onKeyDown` and add a check for `e.nativeEvent.isComposing` to guard against IME composition: `if (e.nativeEvent.isComposing) return`.

title: solutions/closing-marcos-gap/agents/orchestrator.py hardcodes model IDs and token limits instead of reading from settings
evidence: Solution orchestrator.py line 32: `model_id="global.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0"`. Backend orchestrator.py line 53: `model_id=settings.BEDROCK_HAIKU_MODEL`. Solution line 33: `max_tokens=4096`. Backend line 54: `max_tokens=settings.ROUTER_MAX_TOKENS_HAIKU` (= 320).
fix: Replace hardcoded model_id and max_tokens values in both `create_orchestrator` and `create_guarded_orchestrator` with `settings.BEDROCK_HAIKU_MODEL` and `settings.ROUTER_MAX_TOKENS_HAIKU`. Also add `from config import settings` import.

title: solutions/closing-marcos-gap/agents/stock_keeper.py hardcodes max_tokens=2048 instead of settings.AGENT_MAX_TOKENS_HAIKU (800)
evidence: Solution stock_keeper.py line 110: `max_tokens=2048`. Backend stock_keeper.py line 120: `max_tokens=settings.AGENT_MAX_TOKENS_HAIKU`.
fix: Change `max_tokens=2048` to `max_tokens=settings.AGENT_MAX_TOKENS_HAIKU` in the solution.

title: solutions/the-ledger/services/agentcore_memory.py missing _SDK_AVAILABLE module-level cache causing repeated warning spam
evidence: Backend agentcore_memory.py lines 99-176 define `_SDK_AVAILABLE` and the guard logic in `_get_sdk_manager`. Solution has no `_SDK_AVAILABLE` variable; its `_get_sdk_manager` retries the import unconditionally.
fix: Add the `_SDK_AVAILABLE` module-level cache to the solution, matching the backend pattern.

title: solutions/the-quiet-search/services/hybrid_search_with_rerank.py inherits from HybridSearchService but backend class is HybridSearch
evidence: solutions/the-quiet-search/services/hybrid_search_with_rerank.py line 27: `from services.hybrid_search import HybridSearchService` (class does not exist in current backend). Line 57: `super().search(query, embedding, limit, vector_weight, fulltext_weight, ef_search)` — 6-arg call incompatible with the backend's 6-kwarg signature.
fix: This file is likely an archive from the pre-refactor module and should be retired or updated to extend the current `HybridSearch` class with the current `search()` signature.

title: Facilitator cleanup guide uses a wrong Aurora cluster identifier
evidence:
aws rds delete-db-cluster \
  --db-cluster-identifier pellier-workshop  # <-- wrong

# pellier-database.yml line 106:
DBClusterIdentifier: !Sub pellier-cluster-${WorkshopId}
fix:
Replace with a dynamic lookup:
CLUSTER_ID=$(aws rds describe-db-clusters --query "DBClusters[?starts_with(DBClusterIdentifier,'pellier-cluster-')].DBClusterIdentifier" --output text)
aws rds delete-db-cluster --db-cluster-identifier "$CLUSTER_ID" --skip-final-snapshot --delete-automated-backups
Or a

title: Facilitator cleanup guide uses a placeholder CloudFormation stack name
evidence: aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name pellier-workshop-instances
fix: Note that Workshop Studio teardown should be performed via the Workshop Studio event console, or instruct the facilitator to find the actual stack name from the AWS CloudFormation console first. Remove or annotate the command as illustrative only.

title: Participant IAM policy PassRole resource scope does not match the actual AgentCore runtime role name
fix:
Update the Resource ARN in iam_policy.json to use a wildcard suffix:
`arn:aws:iam::*:role/pellier-agentcore-runtime-*`
This covers every WorkshopId variant without broadening scope further.

title: AgentCoreControl Sid in participant IAM policy is entirely redundant with the AgentCoreRuntime wildcard Sid
evidence:
# static/iam_policy.json Sid AgentCoreRuntime (lines 26-30):
"Action": "bedrock-agentcore:*",
"Resource": "*"

# static/iam_policy.json Sid AgentCoreControl (lines 32-49):
"Action": [
  "bedrock-agentcore:CreateAgentRuntime",
  "bedrock-agentcore:GetAgentRuntime",
  ...13 actions...
],
"Resource": "*"
fix: Remove the `AgentCoreControl` Sid entirely. The wildcard in `AgentCoreRuntime` already covers all those actions. If the policy is ever tightened, replace the wildcard with the enumerated list at that time.

But grep confirms @app.entrypoint is only at pellier/backend/agentcore_runtime.py:33, not in services/agentcore_runtime.py.

The optional logger.info edit later in the page correctly targets services/agentcore_runtime.py for run_agent_on_runtime() — that part is correct and should

title: $PG_URL undefined in bootstrap — psql shortcut command fails
evidence:
content/...02-agentcore-runtime/index.en.md line 204: `psql "$PG_URL" -f solutions/the-ledger/sql/tool_audit_recap.sql`
Grep across all .sh and .py files in scripts/ returns zero results for 'PG_URL'. bootstrap-labs.sh defines DATABASE_URL (line 138) but not PG_URL.
fix: Replace `psql "$PG_URL"` with bare `psql` (which picks up PGHOST/PGUSER/PGPASSWORD/PGDATABASE set by bootstrap) or `psql "$DATABASE_URL"`. Update the matching run-instruction comment in solutions/the-ledger/sql/tool_audit_recap.sql as well.

title: Shell alias commands ('backend', 'workshop') in multi-line bash blocks may mislead copy-paste users
evidence:

backend
grep AGENTCORE_RUNTIME_ENDPOINT .env
```

workshop
python3 solutions/the-concierge/mcp_handshake.py
```

fix: Expand the aliases to their full cd paths in multi-line bash blocks, e.g.: 'cd /workshop/sample-pellier-agentic-search-apg/pellier/backend && grep AGENTCORE_RUNTIME_ENDPOINT .env'. Alternatively add a comment noting these are shell aliases requiring an interactive session.

title: gen_placeholders.py: OUT path hard-coded to a dead session mount

fix: Replace with a runtime-computed or configurable path, e.g. `OUT = Path(__file__).parent.parent / 'static'` (the repo-relative location that the README describes), or accept `--output` via argparse and default to the repo root's `static/`.

title: gen_placeholders.py: Linux-only DejaVu font paths crash on macOS/Windows
evidence:
SERIF_ITALIC = "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/DejaVuSerif-Italic.ttf"
SANS = "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/DejaVuSans.ttf"
SANS_BOLD = "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/DejaVuSans-Bold.ttf"
MONO = "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/DejaVuSansMono.ttf"
fix: Add a platform-aware font resolver that tries the Debian path, then the AL2023/RPM path (`/usr/share/fonts/dejavu-sans-fonts/`), then falls back to PIL's built-in `ImageFont.load_default()` with a warning. Alternatively, bundle a copy of DejaVu into `scripts/fonts/` and reference it relatively.
